title: Sound Mnemonic
text: Sound Mnemonic Used to emit a sound, typically a beep. Arguments Mnemonic Explanation buzz Emit a beep. Description If a single command can tell the device to beep several times, include %p in the output sequence. Otherwise, Uniface activates the command ‘ p ’ times. Most devices use ASCII character 007 to beep. The following example shows the definition for the standard VT display: ^007 ^BUZZ Copy Instead of using the character code (^007), you can use the ^BEL mnemonic. For example: ^bel ^BUZZ Copy Related Links Mnemonics for Nonprintable ASCII The control codes required by devices and function key sequences on keyboards often contain nonprintable ASCII characters (with values between 0 and 32 decimal) such as ^ESC, ^SOH, ^NUL, and so on. The translation table compiler recognizes special mnemonics for nonprintable ASCII characters. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
